summary.intent: Address a batch of Codex review comments on PR #665 in fogodev/ars-ui, covering keyboard routing, close callbacks, deselect filtering, aria-describedby ordering, and Set::All hidden-input sentinel.
summary.outcome: All six review items were fixed, tests passed (including ignored proptests and clippy), and the changes were committed as 'fix(components): harden select keyboard and all selection' and pushed to the PR branch.

repo: The select component's trigger keydown handler in crates/ars-components/src/selection/select/mod.rs must check open-state commit cases (Enter/Space while open) before closed-state open cases, otherwise the open trigger never reaches the item-selection branch.
Closed trigger Space must be excluded from the printable-character typeahead branch to prevent queuing TypeaheadSearch events.
The aria-describedby attribute on the trigger should be error-first ordering.
Set::All requires a non-colliding hidden-input sentinel value.
Close callbacks should fire only on actual open-state transitions, not unconditionally.
Deselect-from-all logic must filter disabled keys.

code.finding: Trigger keydown routing section; open-state commit branch must precede closed-state open branch.
Close callback and open-state change logic.
aria-describedby construction; error descriptor should come first.
Listbox deselect logic affected by the disabled-key filter change.
Snapshot required update after aria-describedby ordering fix; accepted via cargo insta accept.

workflow: After Codex posts a review batch, reply to the specific comment thread with the fix commit SHA and resolve the GraphQL review thread in the same shell invocation.
Use 'gh pr comment <number> --body @codex review' to re-trigger Codex after pushing fixes.

Verification chain order: cargo +nightly fmt --check, cargo test -p <crate> <filter>, PROPTEST_CASES=256 cargo test --test proptest_state_machines <filter> -- --ignored, cargo check --features i18n, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.
cargo insta accept is needed when a snapshot legitimately changes due to a review fix before committing.

friction: The Enter/Space closed-trigger open branch was initially ordered before the open-state commit branch, causing a routing regression that required a separate debugging pass to identify and fix.
Multiple rounds of sleep-and-poll were needed to wait for CI stages and Codex review to complete, adding significant wall-clock latency to the session.
open_items: Codex review was still in progress (👀) at the point the session transcript ends; further review rounds may produce additional feedback.
Full CI pipeline status after the final push was not confirmed in the transcript.
